name: Hibevor Light Infantry
notes: Fast infantry movement type. Fielded in squads of 6-10. Usually one has leadership skill. Use jet-packs and personal shields. May use any human weapons (except Ferrapur sniper cannon) plus one additional point per item for weapon modifications.

name: Hibevor Power Armoured Infantry
notes: Assault Dreadnought equivalent — equivalent to dreadnought armour for damage purposes, but troop movement is as if wearing power armour. May act independently.

name: Growwlan Armoured Infantry
notes: Power armoured equivalent veteran trooper. Armed with shock rifle, power sword and shock grenade pack.

name: Growwlan Infantry Chief
notes: Power armoured veteran leader troop. Leadership skill. Armed with shock pistol, shock grenade pack and power sword.

name: Growwlan 'Wrok' Warbird
notes: Assault Dreadnought armoured equivalent animal — glancing hit results ignored. May carry up to 2 Growwlan infantry at once. Warbird rider may fire BOTH forward mounted gun and any personal weapon. Arc of fire same as vehicle mounted forward firing gun (template 3 for arc angle).

name: Ferrapur Armoured Infantry
notes: Light armoured, advanced laser rifle, advanced grenade pack, veteran troop class. Fielded in units of 3-6. Hero skill of Gun Skill (with advanced laser rifle) giving +3 bonus to hit.

name: Ferrapur Marksman
notes: Elite light infantry sniper — only troop class allowed to use the Ferrapur advanced sniper cannon. Also has stealth cloak, advanced laser pistol for close range work, and laser painter device. May act independently.

name: Ferrapur Commander
notes: Power armoured Hero level troop with leadership, swordsman, and Martial Arts skills.

name: Centaling Independent Mercenary
notes: Light armoured equivalent elite troop. May be armed with any infantry/support weapons from human charts, SPUG charts or any advanced laser/grenade pack. Each individual has Scout and Sapper skills. Acts independently.

name: Thuntra Armoured Infantry
notes: Power armoured regular trooper. Arranged in fighting groups of 1-10, one of which must have leadership skill. Carry heavy bolt pistol and heavy advanced grenade pack. Human squad support weapons may be used with additional points cost of 2 for weapon modifications. Also carry heavy power axe.

name: SPUG Infantry Drone
notes: Equipped with shard rifle or carbine (recommended), or may be given weapons from standard infantry weapons list. Usually 4 drones in a pack of 6 (plus leader and heavy weapon drone).

name: SPUG Heavy Weapon Drone
notes: Support Spug. Usually one per pack. Armed with missile launcher, squad support laser, or Toxcannon (recommended — dual-barrelled combining plasma gun and grenade launcher). Either weapon barrel may be fired in a phase, not both.

name: SPUG Heavy Infantry
notes: Spug Power Armour suit. Trained to use Spug Power Armour — classed as light armour but takes damage as if assault dreadnought armour. Organised in packs of 6 heavy infantry, one of which has leadership skill.

name: SPUG Comms Drone
notes: Sophisticated comms equipment. Armed with shard carbine and grenade launcher. Counts as a support targetter, laser painter and scout troop characteristic. Attached to a pack instead of a normal drone trooper.

name: SPUG Alpha Drone Leader
notes: Pack leader. Usually armed with shard carbine with targetter. Has leadership skill. May act independently, but usually leads 5 other drones.

name: SPUG Omega Drone Leader
notes: Pack leader drone excelling in close combat. Often armed with a power claw and shard carbine. Has leadership skill. May act independently, but usually leads 5 other drones.

name: SPUG Theta Drone Leader
notes: Highest class pack leader drone — Hero with skills of leadership, martial arts, initiative and medical. Carries a power glaive which also functions as a shard rifle.

name: Spugnaught (Exo-Suit)
notes: Light Mecha battlesuit encapsulating a mortally wounded Alpha drone. Fitted with power claws plus shard cannons and missile launchers. Slow but fearsome. Acts independently. Filed and fielded exactly as light Mecha/battlesuit.

name: Grey Close Scout
notes: Grey alien in form fitting suit equivalent to light armour. Invisibility cloak. Regular troop class. Carries paralysis rod (acts as power sword in melee — winner paralyses enemy for rest of game). May act independently.

name: Grey Support Scout (P-Beam)
notes: Grey alien with invisibility cloak. Carries spherical P-beam which functions exactly as a P-beam rifle with targetter. The ranged combat trooper of the Grey army. May act independently.

name: Grey Support Scout (Death Ray)
notes: Grey alien with invisibility cloak. Carries energy 'death ray' figures which count as carrying an anti-grav sun cannon (35pts). This unique weapon costs 35pts. When using HOF figures, heavy support death ray counts as carrying anti-grav sun cannon.

name: Grey Control Scout
notes: Grey alien with invisibility cloak. Carries control unit for the Grey Combat Construct. Functions in a squad with along 3 combat constructs. If killed, constructs become suppressed and attempt to move to nearest Grey mothership.

name: Grey Leader
notes: Grey alien in form fitting suit equivalent to assault dreadnought. Null shield fitted. Ambidextrous — carries spherical P-beam and paralysis rod. Has laser painter. Acts independently. Hero skills of agile, constitution and leadership. Coordinates all hostile action/skirmish.

name: Grey Combat Construct
notes: Advanced droid. Power armour class. P-beam rifle, support grenade pack. Veteran troop class. Invisibility cloak. Operates in a coherent squad of 3 droids — must stay within 3 inches of control scout or becomes suppressed.

name: Grey Light Saucer
notes: 3-4m diameter scout saucer with 2 linked P-beam rifles which may fire in any direction as if in a turret. Sun cannon equivalent also mounted. Energy shield mounted. Treated as a fast light antigrav tank. May optionally move as an aircraft. POINTS COST: 130.

name: Bug Runner
notes: Small dog-sized beetle with hard armoured shell. Razor sharp pincers and fast movement. Claws count as melee weapon (no combat modifier, straight 1d10 rolled). Counts as fanatic trooper. Glancing Hit results ignored. Fielded in units of 4-10. No ranged attack.

name: Bug Hunter
notes: 4-6ft tall, chitinous humanoid type hunting alien. Uses stealth and cunning. Has a level of cover one higher than normal. Can engage in close combat if within 4 inches. Fearsome claws and jaws count as power sword. Counts as fanatic trooper. Glancing Hit ignored. Acts independently.

name: Bug Terror
notes: 6-8ft tall, heavily armoured and deadly in close combat. Claws and tail count as power sword in melee. May also spit venom/acid for ranged STANDARD damage attack using template 2 (7+ to hit, 4 inch max range). Fanatic trooper. Glancing Hit ignored. Fielded in units of 3-8.

name: Bug Flyer
notes: Same as Terror class but with rudimentary leathery wings allowing limited flight and gliding. Improved movement allowance. Harder to hit than Terror. Otherwise per Terror class.

name: Bug Cerebral
notes: 20ft long slug-like leader alien with telepathic leadership skill. Poor ground movement but can burrow at 6"/move through any non-concreted/metal-clad terrain. Ranged attack is barb shooter (as SMG, template 2, base 7). Special: Swallow Your Soul attack (3+ on 1d6 against figures it detects above it while burrowed).

name: Bug Queen
notes: Occasionally mutates from large Terror bugs. Has leadership skill. Counts as a terror for combat. Only 1 fielded per army. May lay 1 egg cluster per turn if 4-6 rolled on 1d6. Will fight if guards have been killed.

name: Bug Colossus
notes: Large 12-15ft tall humanoid walking nightmare. Classed as hull class 3. Tail attacks and two poison barb shooters may be fired at different targets (+5 H2H bonus equivalent to power axe). Fanatic trooper. Glancing Hit ignored. May act independently. Range 4 inches close combat.

name: Bug Egg Cluster
notes: Stationary pulsating acid-mucous coated eggs waiting to hatch. May be purchased and placed at game start, or laid by queen Bug. Roll 1d6 each full game turn — roll of 5-6 places a runner bug next to cluster; roll of 6 allows placing a terror bug. Hatched bugs must immediately join nearest squad.

name: Replican Enforcer
notes: Base model Replican attack trooper. Fielded in squads of 4-10 figures.

name: Replican CPU Director
notes: Advanced trooper with comprehensive leadership and communications package. Constantly in contact with Replican CPC. At least one must be fielded per army. Leadership skill.

name: Replican Heavy Battle Droid
notes: Replican version of the Imperial Heavy Battle Droid. Identical in operation to robots section — also note this is a robot so may malfunction.

name: Replican Mimick
notes: Humanoid shaped Replican android with biological skin layer. Moves as unit it's camouflaged within. Up to 3 Mimicks per battle. Activated at start of Replican fire phase — roll 1d6, 5-6 activates and joins a random enemy squad. Gets first shot then revealed. Cannot be placed in enemy Replican, Grey or Hibevor armies. Has built-in palm flamer and power glove.

name: Replican Shroud
notes: Stealth-camouflaged, light armoured bipedal or quadrupedal unit designed to close in on enemy quickly and silently. Used for assassinations and designation missions.

name: Replican Artilleroid
notes: Functions exactly as the Robo-Artillery unit in the robots section.

name: K'Kree Hindmost Leader
notes: Herd leader with quadrupedal power armour. Usually armed with heavy weapons. Leadership skill.

name: K'Kree Hindmost Staff
notes: Bodyguards to the Leader. Fast infantry class. Often found with melee weapons.

name: K'Kree Light Infantry
notes: Standard Veteran fast attack trooper with weapon of choice. K'Kree fast infantry — excellent open-ground movement. All military K'Kree are at least Veteran class.

name: K'Kree Light Armour
notes: Standard Veteran K'Kree in light armour with weapon of choice. All military K'Kree are at least Veteran class.
